overview: Izzet Delver is a reliable Legacy staple that applies early pressure through low-cost spells and consistent card advantage. The updated list integrates Cori-Steel Cutter to generate oppressive token swarms while maintaining its classic disruption and tempo game plan. It remains a safe haven in the format, capable of adapting to various meta shifts without losing its core identity.
win_conditions: Transforming Delver of Secrets into a 3/2 flying creature
Generating token swarms with Cori-Steel Cutter
Casting rapid low-cost spells to apply early pressure

keep_criteria: Inclusion of card draw spells like Brainstorm or Ponder
Early pressure or disruption like Wasteland
Reactive cards paired with cantrips to find answers
mulligan_criteria: Hands with 3 lands unless accompanied by Wasteland or strong action
Overly reactive hands lacking early pressure
Hands without card draw to filter or find threats

examples.reason: Has protection, card draw, disruption, and can fit Murktide Regent early despite lacking a turn 1 creature.
Provides pressure for the first two turns, Brainstorm finds protection/action, and fetchland shuffles dead cards.
Lacks card draw to filter, relies solely on Channeler, and Wasteland could ruin the match if lands are needed.
Brazen Borrower offers defense, Baubles ensure Delver transforms, and stack manipulation at upkeep optimizes the draw.

early_game: Play low-cost spells to apply immediate pressure
Use Wasteland to disrupt opponent's mana base
Hold up Force of Will or Daze for protection
mid_game: Transform Delver of Secrets using Baubles or card draw
Activate Cori-Steel Cutter to generate tokens on both turns
Utilize Murktide Regent's Delve and Dragon's Rage Channeler's Delirium
late_game: Maintain board presence with token swarms
Continue cycling through card draw to find answers
Adapt sideboard choices based on meta threats

key_cards.name: Delver of Secrets
key_cards.notes: Transforms into a 3/2 flying creature to close out games.

key_cards.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
key_cards.notes: Creates oppressive tokens that trigger on both turns when casting spells.

key_cards.name: Dragon's Rage Channeler
key_cards.notes: Allows filtering the top card and enables Delirium for Mishra's Bauble.

key_cards.name: Force of Will
key_cards.notes: Essential counter spell to protect early threats and key plays.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.notes: Core cantrip to find lands, threats, and answers while maintaining tempo.

key_cards.name: Wasteland
key_cards.notes: Destroys nonbasic lands to hinder opponent's strategy.

key_cards.name: Mishra's Bauble
key_cards.notes: Provides card draw and enables Delirium for Channeler and Regent.

key_cards.name: Urza's Bauble
key_cards.notes: Complements Mishra's Bauble, fuels Cori-Steel Cutter, and enables Delve/Delirium.

sequencing_tips: Put Cori-Steel Cutter on the board on turn 2 and immediately follow up with a 0-cost spell to trigger its ability.
Activate Mishra's Bauble at the end of the opponent's turn to trigger Delirium before your turn begins.
When using Brazen Borrower, set up the stack at your upkeep to reveal the top card before drawing it if it's decent, or draw first if it isn't.
Prioritize casting spells on the opponent's turn to maximize Cori-Steel Cutter's token generation.

matchups.advice: Keep some Force of Will instead of removing all, as Barrowgoyf is a major threat. Use Murktide Regent's Delve to control its size.
Race to prevent them from resolving Show and Tell or Sneak Attack. Add more counters and remove Lightning Bolt post-side.
Delver is slightly favored. Use Wasteland on Ancient Tomb. Note that Daze is less reliable due to Simian Spirit Guide.
Lists vary between blue/tempo and monoblack. Adjust sideboard accordingly, focusing on graveyard hate and counters.
Kill Painter's Servant immediately to prevent combo and amplified Blasts. Cori-Steel Cutter performs better than Murktide Regent here.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Delver is Legacy's safe haven. Even when it isn't the best deck, it is always reliable and can beat any other archetype in the format.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: get another shot to evolve your 1/1 into a 3/2
both create tokens that can become oppressive quite quickly
play many low-cost spells in a row and put pressure on the opponent

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: the best way to use it is to put it on the board on turn 2 and follow up with a 0-cost spell.
you'll activate one of them at the end of the opponent's turn.
set up the stack at your upkeep to reveal it before you draw it. If it isn't, draw it before you reveal it
Cutter's ability triggers on both turns, so casting 2 spells on your opponent's turn will give you another token.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: The key card in this matchup is Barrowgoyf.
prevent them from resolving Show and Tell or Sneak Attack
Chalice of the Void and Blood Moon can be disruptive
Wasteland is still brutal when you use it on an Ancient Tomb
they often play around it with Simian Spirit Guide
Painter's Servant needs to die as soon as possible
